zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert Powell於1508年出生在英國素福爾可米爾登哈爾時，他的父親William Powell30歲，母親Agnes Peche33歲。他於1528年與Johanne Dobson在英國素福爾可哈德利結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1588年3月23日在英國素福爾可米爾登哈爾享年80歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 布利德也斯 邱奇 福利特 史崔特。

nameMeanings.name: Powell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Hywel ‘son of Hywel’, a personal name meaning ‘eminent’ (see Howell ).
Irish: mainly of Welsh origin as in 1 above, but sometimes a surname adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Giolla Phóil ‘son of the servant of Saint Paul’ (see Guilfoyle ).
English: variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .

variants.name: Powel
meanings: North German: from a form of the personal name   Paul  .

variants.name: Howell
meanings: Welsh and English: in the Welsh border counties, from the Welsh male personal name Hywel ‘eminent’, popular since the Middle Ages particularly in honor of the great 10th-century law-giving Welsh king. The name may also arise from the Breton personal name Houuel, Huwel, Huwal, Howael, cognate with the Welsh name. Bretons were especially numerous in the eastern counties of England. See Powell .
English: perhaps from a Middle English personal name Huwel (spelled Howel), a shortening of Huwelot or Huelin (see Hewlett and Huling ), both pet forms of Middle English Huw (see Hugh ).
English: habitational name from Howell (Lincolnshire), from an uncertain initial element + Old English wella ‘well, spring, stream’.

variants.name: Powles
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Powells, itself a variant of Powell , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Powell was born in 1508, in Mildenhall, Suffolk, England, his father, William Powell, was 30 and his mother, Agnes Peche, was 33. He married Johanne Dobson in 1528, in Hadleigh, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 23 March 1588, in Mildenhall, Suffolk, England, at the age of 80, and was buried in St Bride's Church Fleet Street, London, England, United Kingdom.

ko.St. Bride's, Fleet Street, London, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 브라이데'스 c후르치 플릿 스트릿
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Powell(이)가 1508년 영국 수폴크 미데홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Powell(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Peche(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1528년 영국 수폴크 하들리에서 Johanne Dobson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1588년 3월 23일 영국 수폴크 미데홀에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 브라이데'스 c후르치 플릿 스트릿에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert Powell родился в 1508 г. в местности маилденолл, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени William Powell было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Peche, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Johanne Dobson в 1528 г. в местности хадлиг, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер 23 марта 1588 г. в местности маилденолл, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 80 лет и был похоронен в местности ст бриде'с чурч флит стрит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
